title: Image Distribution and Upgrade Progress Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detects devices running software versions that deviate from the Catalyst Center golden image baseline.
compliance.controlObjective: Identifies firmware non-compliance against the approved software baseline.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search UC-5.13.56 scheduled daily; non-compliant device list exported to change management dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Supports flaw remediation by tracking firmware version compliance and upgrade progress.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that devices are being maintained on approved software versions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Firmware compliance percentage trending report; upgrade success/failure dashboard.

dataSources: Catalyst Center Intent API SWIM distribution status via custom scripted input or webhooks â†’ `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for SWIM API
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" upgradeStatus=* | stats count by upgradeStatus, deviceFamily | eval status_order=case(upgradeStatus="SUCCESS",1,upgradeStatus="IN_PROGRESS",2,upgradeStatus="SCHEDULED",3,upgradeStatus="FAILED",4,1==1,5) | sort status_order -count
description: Tracks the progress of firmware distribution and upgrade operations across the managed device fleet, showing success, in-progress, scheduled, and failed states.
value: Large-scale firmware upgrades are high-risk operations. Real-time progress tracking ensures failures are caught immediately and upgrade windows are respected.
implementation:
Ingest `upgradeStatus` (and related metadata) from Catalyst Center SWIM into `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim` using the same poller as UC-5.13.55. Primary Intent API: `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` (often returns schedule, state, and device identifiers; field names may include versions of SUCCESS, IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, etc.). Re-authenticate on each run or cache the token with expiry handling via `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token`.

**Scripted input example** (`interval` aligned to your change window, for example 300â€“900 seconds during upgrades):

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_swim/bin/collect_swim.py]
interval = 300
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:swim
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

**HEC / event notifications (optional):** For faster state transitions, register Catalyst Center event notifications (if your release exposes SWIM task events) to Splunk HEC and merge into the same sourcetype or a secondary `cisco:dnac:swim:event` that you `union` in dashboards.
visualization: Stacked bar or column chart of count by `upgradeStatus` and `deviceFamily`, single values for in-progress and failed, timeline if `_time` from event notifications is used.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ UC-5.13.55 live with SWIM data in `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`.

Step 1 â€” API and fields
Poll `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` after `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token`. Normalize the APIâ€™s state strings into a field `upgradeStatus` (for example map Catalyst Centerâ€™s enumeration to `SUCCESS`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `SCHEDULED`, `FAILED` for consistent SPL). Include `deviceFamily` in each event. If the API returns nested tasks, either flatten in the script or index one event per (device, task).

Step 2 â€” Real-time path
If you enable Platform event notifications to HEC, set the HEC `sourcetype` to `cisco:dnac:swim` or a parallel sourcetype and add an `| union` in dashboards, or use `collect` in an alert to a summary index for trending.

Step 3 â€” Search

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" upgradeStatus=* | stats count by upgradeStatus, deviceFamily | eval status_order=case(upgradeStatus="SUCCESS",1,upgradeStatus="IN_PROGRESS",2,upgradeStatus="SCHEDULED",3,upgradeStatus="FAILED",4,1==1,5) | sort status_order -count
```

Step 4 â€” Validate
Trigger a test upgrade in a lab and confirm `upgradeStatus` transitions and counts match Catalyst Center SWIM job UI.

Step 5 â€” Operationalize
Use during change windows: dashboard refresh 1â€“5 min, optional alerts on rising `FAILED` or stalled `IN_PROGRESS` beyond an SLA window.
grandmaExplanation: We track the progress of software updates across your entire network, showing which devices have been updated, which are in progress, and which failed.
